jokes.text: IT SCARES ME. [awkward silence after overly intense romantic declaration]
jokes.setup: Typical teen couple conversation about relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic confession followed by uncomfortable silence
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - the silence does all the comedic work. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: HE PLAYS DRUMS FOR LED ZEPPELIN, AND HIS NAME IS JOHN BONHAM, BABY!
jokes.setup: Daniel explaining his religious beliefs after being kicked out of church
jokes.punchline: God is John Bonham from Led Zeppelin
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that perfectly captures Daniel's burnout logic and musical obsession. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: SAM QUEER. IT'S FIGHTIN' TIME, WEIRD.
jokes.setup: Alan picking on Sam about liking Bill Murray
jokes.punchline: Crude wordplay on Sam's last name
jokes.explanation: Basic bully humor - predictable wordplay but establishes Alan's character as crude antagonist.

jokes.text: I ALWAYS WANTED TO KNOW WHAT IT'D BE LIKE TO FIGHT A GIRL.
jokes.setup: Alan threatening Sam, a smaller kid
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently calling Sam a girl
jokes.explanation: Solid setup-punchline that works because Alan doesn't realize he just called Sam a girl.

jokes.text: I'M A GIRL. WANNA SEE WHAT IT'D BE LIKE TO FIGHT ME?
jokes.setup: Alan's comment about fighting a girl
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's direct challenge using his own words
jokes.explanation: Perfect comeback that turns Alan's threat back on him. Great economy and character moment for Lindsay.

jokes.text: I THINK SHE'S HIGH ON POT.
jokes.setup: Lindsay standing up to Alan
jokes.punchline: Alan's clueless diagnosis of why she's being assertive
jokes.explanation: Shows Alan's limited understanding of the world - any assertive behavior must be drugs.

jokes.text: AND BY THE WAY, I WEIGH 103 POUNDS. SORRY.
jokes.setup: Lindsay defending Sam from bullies who pick on small kids
jokes.punchline: Sam's specific correction of his tiny weight
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Sam's awkward precision about his own smallness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: MAN, I HATE HIGH SCHOOL.
jokes.setup: Sam just survived a bullying encounter thanks to his sister
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement after traumatic experience
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective understatement that captures the teenage experience.

jokes.text: HE'S DEAD. LET'S GO DIG HIM UP NOW AND SEE HOW COOL HE LOOKS.
jokes.setup: Harold warning about smoking dangers
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they literally dig up his dead friend
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Harold's extreme parenting style. Dark humor that escalates to absurdity while staying in character.

jokes.text: THEY'RE JUST A CHANCE FOR THE POPULAR KIDS TO EXPERIMENT WITH SEX IN THEIR CARS.
jokes.setup: Parents encouraging kids to go to school dance
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's blunt assessment of what dances really are
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that shows Lindsay's cynicism and intelligence.

jokes.text: DIED... OF AN OVERDOSE-- HEROIN. DAD, ARE ANY OF YOUR FRIENDS ALIVE?
jokes.setup: Harold's second cautionary tale about premarital sex
jokes.punchline: Sam's exasperated question about Harold's mortality rate among friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that addresses Harold's pattern of death-related warnings. Excellent economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: THE SMART ONES.
jokes.setup: Sam asking if any of Harold's friends are alive
jokes.punchline: Harold's matter-of-fact response implying all his friends who made bad choices died
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that continues the running gag while staying completely in character.

jokes.text: I'M HAVING A MIDNIGHT MADNESS DEER HUNTING SALE THAT NIGHT, AND YOU CAN COME WAIT ON ALL THOSE NICE HUNTERS.
jokes.setup: Harold giving Lindsay choice between dance and work
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific alternative of serving deer hunters at midnight
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that shows Harold's specific world and parenting style.

jokes.text: IT'S SCHWEIBER.
jokes.setup: Student mispronouncing teacher's name
jokes.punchline: Teacher's resigned correction
jokes.explanation: Simple running gag that feels authentic to school experience.

jokes.text: [Bill doing terrible William Shatner impression] I'M SO HUNGRY I COULD EAT A TRIBBLE.
jokes.setup: Bill attempting celebrity impressions
jokes.punchline: Completely off-base Shatner impression with Star Trek reference
jokes.explanation: Shows Bill's nerdy enthusiasm and poor social skills through bad impressions.

jokes.text: JOHN WAYNE.
jokes.setup: Friends asking who Bill is impersonating
jokes.punchline: Neal's completely wrong guess
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that shows the geeks' pop culture confusion.

jokes.text: AT LEAST YOUR MOM DOESN'T WRITE THE NOTE ON THE BAG.
jokes.setup: Sam complaining about mom putting notes in his lunch
jokes.punchline: Neal's worse situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows the hierarchy of lunch embarrassment.

jokes.text: OH, MY GOD. YOU'RE SUCH A WOMAN.
jokes.setup: Sam reporting Alan to the lunch monitor
jokes.punchline: Alan's crude gendered insult
jokes.explanation: Basic bully humor that establishes Alan's character but lacks sophistication.

jokes.text: BE A MAN.
jokes.setup: Teacher giving Sam advice about bullying
jokes.punchline: Unhelpful masculine advice to a tiny kid
jokes.explanation: Shows the useless adult advice teens receive, especially ironic given Sam's size.

jokes.text: YOUR BUTT WAS CALLING TO ME.
jokes.setup: Ken inappropriately touching someone and apologizing
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous excuse
jokes.explanation: Establishes Ken's crude character but relies on cheap sexual humor.

jokes.text: YOU'RE THAT CHICK WHO GOT AN 'A.'
jokes.setup: Nick recognizing Lindsay from English class
jokes.punchline: Reducing her entire identity to getting good grades
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing how the freaks view academic achievement as strange.

jokes.text: WHO'S YOUR DAD-- HITLER?
jokes.setup: Lindsay saying her dad is making her go to the dance
jokes.punchline: Ken's extreme comparison
jokes.explanation: Typical teenage hyperbole that fits Ken's character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: I'D RATHER MAKE OUT WITH PRINCIPAL FARBER. OOH, AGAIN.
jokes.setup: Daniel expressing hatred of disco
jokes.punchline: Extreme comparison followed by Ken's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor with effective reaction beat from Ken.

jokes.text: BUT YOU'RE OUR BEST MATH-LETE.
jokes.setup: Millie trying to convince Lindsay to join academic decathlon
jokes.punchline: The cringe-worthy portmanteau 'math-lete'
jokes.explanation: The cringe factor comes from how earnestly Millie uses this terrible word.

jokes.text: 'She brought you your jacket. That means...' elaborate analysis
jokes.setup: Cindy returning Sam's jacket
jokes.punchline: Overanalysis of simple kind gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect geek overthinking, builds romantic hope beautifully

jokes.text: NO WAY. WE'RE NOT HER SPECIES.
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether Cindy likes Sam
jokes.punchline: Bill's biological comparison of social groups
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that captures high school social dynamics through Bill's scientific mindset.

jokes.text: YOU'VE SEEN STAR WARS 27 TIMES. DO THE MATH.
jokes.setup: Sam thinking he has a chance with cheerleader Cindy
jokes.punchline: Bill pointing out the obvious social reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of geek/popular divide with specific character detail.

jokes.text: MAYBE I SHOULD ASK OUT BILL'S MOM. YOU BETTER NOT.
jokes.setup: Joking about asking out unattainable women
jokes.punchline: Bill's serious response to the mom joke
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that shows Bill taking the joke seriously.

jokes.text: 'Why don't you blow your nose in some bread and make me a sandwich, too?'
jokes.setup: Ken using his gum wrapper inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Disgusting escalation of gross behavior
jokes.explanation: Effective gross-out humor, shows Kim's aggressive personality

jokes.text: WHY DON'T YOU BLOW YOUR NOSE IN SOME BREAD AND MAKE ME A SANDWICH, TOO?
jokes.setup: Daniel offering Kim used gum
jokes.punchline: Kim's disgusted escalation
jokes.explanation: Great gross-out escalation that establishes Kim's aggressive personality.

jokes.text: WHAT, ARE YOU DOING HER SO THAT SHE'LL HELP YOU WITH YOUR MATH HOMEWORK?
jokes.setup: Kim questioning why Lindsay is hanging with the freaks
jokes.punchline: Crude assumption about Daniel and Lindsay's relationship
jokes.explanation: Shows Kim's hostility and crude worldview but relies on cheap sexual humor.

jokes.text: I SHOPLIFT IN YOUR DADDY'S STORE.
jokes.setup: Kim attacking Lindsay as a rich kid poser
jokes.punchline: Admitting to stealing from Lindsay's family business
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that perfectly captures Kim's antagonistic character and class resentment.

jokes.text: [Kim spits in Lindsay's face] THERE. NOW WE'RE FRIENDS. SEE YOU AT THE MALL.
jokes.setup: Kim pretending to make peace with Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Spitting in her face while claiming friendship
jokes.explanation: Shocking physical comedy paired with sarcastic dialogue creates memorable moment.

jokes.text: THAT IS EXACTLY WHY I DON'T CARRY A PURSE.
jokes.setup: Kim just spit in Lindsay's face and stormed off
jokes.punchline: Daniel's completely unrelated observation about purses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant non-sequitur that shows Daniel's stoned logic and provides comic relief after intense moment.

jokes.text: SHE'S BEEN ACTING WEIRD EVER SINCE OUR GRANDMA DIED.
jokes.setup: Cindy asking about Lindsay's changed behavior
jokes.punchline: Sam's matter-of-fact explanation involving death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Sam's casual delivery of serious information to explain weird behavior.

jokes.text: I KIND OF WISH I DIDN'T COME TO SCHOOL TODAY.
jokes.setup: Sam being terrorized by Alan all day
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of his horrible day
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement that captures Sam's perpetual victimhood.

jokes.text: WHAT IF HE WAS MY HUSBAND? THAT'D BE SOME KIND OF JOKE TO YOU?
jokes.setup: Students making fun of her and Eli as a couple
jokes.punchline: Her defensive escalation to marriage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows character defending relationship against mockery.

jokes.text: PLAY DODGE BALL. [collective groans]
jokes.setup: Coach announcing 'fun' dodgeball game
jokes.punchline: Students' horrified reaction to 'fun' activity
jokes.explanation: Universal school experience - adults thinking dodgeball is fun while kids dread it.

jokes.text: WOULD SOMEBODY PLEASE TELL ME WHAT'S SUPPOSED TO BE FUN ABOUT THIS?
jokes.setup: Dodgeball being called fun by adults
jokes.punchline: Neal's existential question about the 'fun' nature of violence
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor that questions adult assumptions about childhood 'fun.'

jokes.text: ONLY MY MOM'S ALLOWED TO CALL ME THAT.
jokes.setup: Alan calling Sam 'little man'
jokes.punchline: Sam's oddly specific boundary about nicknames
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Sam's weird priorities even when being bullied.

jokes.text: DODGE BALL'S KIND OF A STUPID GAME, ISN'T IT?
jokes.setup: Sam is the last geek standing in brutal dodgeball game
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about the game's stupidity while being hunted
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan moment - casual philosophical observation during intense physical threat.

jokes.text: PRESIDENT ELI! [awkward political discussion with disabled student]
jokes.setup: Students pretending to have serious political discussion with Eli
jokes.punchline: The transparent mockery disguised as inclusion
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable cringe humor that exposes cruel teenage behavior.

jokes.text: I'M NOT RETARDED. I'M SPECIAL.
jokes.setup: Lindsay accidentally using hurtful language
jokes.punchline: Eli's dignified correction
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking moment that shows Eli's dignity and Lindsay's good intentions gone wrong.

jokes.text: AND I'M THE MEAN ONE? HOW DOES THAT WORK?
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to help Eli, resulting in him getting hurt and upset
jokes.punchline: Her frustration at being seen as the villain
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation about how good intentions can backfire.

jokes.text: OH, GOD, PLEASE DON'T SAY THAT WORD.
jokes.setup: Counselor using the word 'math-lete'
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's visceral reaction to the cringey term
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier 'math-lete' cringe, showing Lindsay's continued horror at the term.

jokes.text: 'Oh, God, please don't say that word.' (about 'math-lete')
jokes.setup: Rosso mentioning academic decathlon
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's visceral reaction to cringey term
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Lindsay's growing distance from academic culture

jokes.text: I'M JUST JEFF, YOUR FRIEND WHO CARES.
jokes.setup: Guidance counselor trying to be relatable to student
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at being the 'cool' adult
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe moment of adult trying too hard to connect with teens.

jokes.text: WELL, THEN MAYBE SOMEONE SHOULD TELL YOUR FACE.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claiming she's never felt better
jokes.punchline: Rosso's surprisingly sharp observation about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly sharp comeback from the usually awkward counselor.

jokes.text: HIS KIND SHOULD BE DESTROYED.
jokes.setup: Rosso giving psychological analysis of bullies
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift from understanding to violent solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection from therapeutic understanding to violent solution - perfectly subverts expectations.

jokes.text: HE BROKE MY TAILBONE, BUT THE RESULTS WERE EFFECTIVE. HE GOT EXPELLED.
jokes.setup: Rosso explaining his anti-bullying strategy from his youth
jokes.punchline: His 'successful' outcome involved serious injury and expulsion
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery of a 'success' story that was actually traumatic.

jokes.text: YOU GOTTA FIND YOUR BIG, GIGANTIC DRUM KIT, YA KNOW?
jokes.setup: Nick explaining his philosophy of finding your passion in life
jokes.punchline: Using his massive drum kit as universal metaphor for life purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Nick's earnest but misguided attempt at wisdom.

jokes.text: HEY, MAYBE I'LL BUY A CLARINET.
jokes.setup: Nick's passionate speech about finding your 'big gigantic drum kit'
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's underwhelming response suggesting a tiny, uncool instrument
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation of Nick's grand metaphor with the most uncool instrument possible.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW WHO USED TO CUT CLASS? JIMI HENDRIX. YOU KNOW WHAT HAPPENED TO HIM? HE DIED, CHOKING ON HIS OWN VOMIT.
jokes.setup: Harold learning Lindsay skipped class
jokes.punchline: Another death-related cautionary tale
jokes.explanation: Continues Harold's running gag of connecting everything to death with rock star reference.

jokes.text: EVERYBODY'S STILL ALIVE. JUST LEAVE ME ALONE!
jokes.setup: Harold's endless death-related warnings
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's exasperated response pointing out the obvious
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Harold's running gag with Lindsay finally calling out the absurdity.

jokes.text: UM, WHY ARE YOU THROWING YOUR LIFE AWAY?
jokes.setup: Sam visiting Lindsay in her room
jokes.punchline: His blunt, childlike directness about her life choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling moment - Sam's innocent directness cuts through all the adult complexity.

jokes.text: 'No. There's nothing.' Grandma's deathbed revelation
jokes.setup: Grandma asked about seeing God/heaven/light while dying
jokes.punchline: Stark nihilistic response about afterlife
jokes.explanation: Devastating existential moment that explains Lindsay's entire character arc

jokes.text: YEAH. HE'S A GONER.
jokes.setup: Sam asking if they could really beat up Alan
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's confident but obviously false assessment
jokes.explanation: Sweet lie that shows Lindsay protecting her brother's confidence.

jokes.text: EITHER WAY. [Colin excited to watch the fight regardless of outcome]
jokes.setup: Colin asked if he came to help or because they'll win
jokes.punchline: His honest admission he just wants to see violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about teenage bloodlust - Colin just wants entertainment.

jokes.text: WE'RE KIND OF HERE TO BEAT YOU UP. BILL!
jokes.setup: Confrontation with Alan, need for element of surprise
jokes.punchline: Bill accidentally revealing their plan
jokes.explanation: Classic Bill moment - his honesty ruins any tactical advantage.

jokes.text: 'I'll save a dance for you.'
jokes.setup: Sam asking Cindy to dance despite her having a date
jokes.punchline: Sweet compromise offer that gives Sam hope
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment that keeps Sam's romantic hopes alive

jokes.text: I GUESS THE NOSE BONE MUST HAVE MISSED MY BRAIN.
jokes.setup: Earlier warning about nose bone being driven into brain during fights
jokes.punchline: Bill's relief at surviving the fight
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier fight warnings with Bill's typical scientific analysis.

jokes.text: I HAD HIS SHOES.
jokes.setup: Everyone claiming their crucial role in the fight
jokes.punchline: Colin's pathetic contribution to the battle
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how everyone wants credit for the 'victory' even with minimal contribution.

jokes.text: NO. WE GOT HIT BY A CAR.
jokes.setup: Sam asking if they fought Alan
jokes.punchline: Revealing they didn't fight at all, just had an accident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that completely reframes the entire 'victory' - perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: MAYBE YOU SHOULD START CALLING ME MR. ROSSO AGAIN.
jokes.setup: Earlier attempt to be 'Jeff, your friend who cares'
jokes.punchline: Rosso retreating back to professional distance
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing how his attempt to be the cool counselor backfired.
